name: Hot Spot
description: You feel that telltale warmth on the ball of your foot. Right now it's a warning. In two miles it'll be a blister.

choices.label: Stop and tape it (45 seconds)
choices.outcome: Quick tape job on the side of the road. It'll hold. Forty-five seconds well spent.

choices.label: Ignore it
choices.outcome: You'll deal with it later. That's what every runner says right before they can't walk.

debuff.name: Severe Blister
debuff.effect: Blood blister — every step is agony

choices.label: Adjust shoes and slow down (20 seconds)
choices.outcome: A quick readjustment. Not perfect, but better than nothing.

name: The Wall
description: Mile 20. Your glycogen is gone. Your legs are concrete. Every cell in your body is begging you to stop. This is the marathon.

choices.label: Dig deep
choices.outcome: You find something inside you didn't know was there. This is what you trained for.

debuff.name: Bonking
debuff.effect: Glycogen depleted — everything is shutting down

choices.label: Walk and regroup (3 minutes)
choices.outcome: Walking lets you gather yourself. Many marathoners walk the wall. No shame in it.

choices.label: Take a gel if you have one
choices.outcome: The gel hits fast. Sugar floods your system. The wall cracks.

name: Stomach Trouble
description: Your stomach is staging a rebellion. Something you ate is fighting its way back up. Every runner knows this feeling.

choices.label: Slow down and sip water (60 seconds)
choices.outcome: The nausea passes after a few minutes. Smart call.

choices.label: Push through
choices.outcome: Bad call. It gets worse before it gets better. Way worse.

debuff.name: GI Distress
debuff.effect: Stomach revolt — can't hold anything down

choices.label: Find a bush (2 minutes)
choices.outcome: Embarrassing but effective. You emerge from the bushes a new person.

name: Muscle Cramp
description: Your calf seizes up mid-stride. The kind of cramp that stops you dead and makes you grab the muscle like it owes you money.

choices.label: Stop and stretch (30 seconds)
choices.outcome: Thirty seconds of stretching on the side of the road. It releases. Keep moving.

choices.label: Run through it
choices.outcome: It loosens eventually, but every step until then is agony.

debuff.name: Cramp
debuff.effect: Muscle seizing up with every stride

choices.label: Use electrolytes if you have them
choices.outcome: The electrolytes work fast. The cramp melts away. Chemistry saves the day.

name: Twisted Ankle
description: Your foot catches a root and your ankle rolls hard. Pain shoots up your leg. You stumble but stay upright.

choices.label: Walk it off (60 seconds)
choices.outcome: Gingerly walking it out. The pain dulls to a throb. You can keep going.

choices.label: Keep running
choices.outcome: You grit your teeth and push through. Every footstrike sends a jolt up your leg.

debuff.name: Ankle Pain
debuff.effect: Shooting pain with every step — not getting better

choices.label: Drop out (DNF)
choices.outcome: Sometimes the smart move is knowing when to stop.
choices.dnfReason: Twisted ankle — pulled out to avoid further injury

name: Dehydration Warning
description: Your mouth is dry, your head is pounding, and the world is getting a little fuzzy around the edges. You need fluids. Now.

choices.label: Drink everything you have
choices.outcome: Water, electrolytes, more water. You feel the fog lift after a few minutes.

choices.label: Ration and keep going
choices.outcome: Small sips. It's not enough. The headache stays.

debuff.name: Dehydrated
debuff.effect: Head pounding, vision fuzzy — need fluids

choices.label: Use a drink if you have one
choices.outcome: The drink goes down smooth. Hydration restored. Crisis averted.

name: Headwind
description: The wind shifts and you're running straight into it. Every step feels like pushing through a wall of air.

name: Perfect Weather
description: Cool breeze, overcast skies, low humidity. Runners call this 'PR weather.' The conditions are doing half the work for you.

name: Second Wind
description: You were hurting. Now you're not. The legs have come back to life and the pace feels easy again.

name: Fellow Runner Down
description: A runner beside you is in rough shape. Hands on knees, breathing hard, eyes unfocused. They look like they might drop.

choices.label: Stop and help (90 seconds)
choices.outcome: You stay with them until help arrives. They look up and nod. 'Thank you.' This is what running is about.

choices.label: Call out encouragement and keep going
choices.outcome: A quick word as you pass. It's something. You hope they make it.

choices.label: Pretend you didn't see
choices.outcome: You run past without looking. You're racing too. But guilt gnaws at you for the next mile.

name: Hallucination
description: The trees are talking to you. You're fine. Probably. A mailbox waves hello. The road breathes. This is ultra running.
